summary: Decompiled sysdolphin baselib rumble from assembly into src/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.c, wired the new C object into the build, added the PADControlMotor prototype needed by the implementation, and registered rumble.o in the epilogue unscheduled object list. The PR had no body or review discussion in the provided slice, so intent and reviewer rationale are inferred only from the diff.
what_changed: Added a new 363-line C implementation for sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.c replacing the previous rumble assembly object in the normal object list.
Defined local rumble-related data types in rumble.c, including RumbleCommand, HSD_Rumble, HSD_PadData, HSD_PadRumbleListData, RumbleInfo, PadLibData, and Struct804C22E0.

Implemented rumble list allocation, insertion by priority, removal, pause/resume, per-controller clearing, global clearing, command interpretation, motor state update, and initialization routines under current symbol names such as HSD_PadRumbleOn, func_803780DC, func_80378430, func_803786F0, and func_80378828.
Added the Dolphin SDK declaration void PADControlMotor(int chan, u32 command); to include/dolphin/pad/pad.h.
Added a blank line before the .data section alignment in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/util.s, likely a harmless formatting or assembly-layout nudge in the adjacent baselib assembly file.
smart_moves: Kept unknown or not-yet-understood symbols as address-derived func_80378xxx and lbl_804xxxxx names rather than overclaiming semantics.
Used explicit struct field offsets in comments for HSD_PadRumbleListData and PadLibData, preserving ABI/layout evidence needed for a matching decompilation.
Modeled HSD_Rumble as a union of a raw u16 definition and a decoded command structure, matching the bit-packed command stream style visible in func_80378560.
Protected mutations of global rumble state and list state with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, matching the interrupt-sensitive nature of pad rumble management.
Factored repeated list operations into helper-style functions with '_inline' names where the matching strategy apparently required separately represented inlineable code patterns.
Preserved dummy stack arrays such as u8 dummy[8] in some functions, a common matching tactic to force stack frame shape when source-level logic alone would not reproduce the target assembly.
Updated both the main object file list and the epilogue unscheduled list, avoiding the common mistake of adding a C file without all required build-system bookkeeping.
Added only the needed PADControlMotor prototype to the pad header instead of introducing broader unrelated SDK declarations.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling baselib modules, local struct definitions can be acceptable if shared headers are not ready, but field offsets should be documented to stabilize later header extraction.
For linked-list manager code, pointer-to-pointer traversal is a useful way to match removal and priority insertion patterns, as shown by func_80378128, func_80378430_inline, and func_803786F0_inline.

Rumble command interpreters may need to represent encoded script words as u16 pointers even when a higher-level union exists; the PR comments show HSD_Rumble pointers were intentionally left as u16* for matching.

Global mutable pad state should be wrapped in interrupt disable/restore calls in the same lexical regions as the original assembly, since moving those calls can alter register allocation and matching.
If a function needs a known name but the symbol is not yet named in the map, a comment such as '// HSD_PadRumbleInit' beside func_80378828 can record likely identity without renaming prematurely.
Small stack-padding locals like dummy arrays should be retained when they are needed for stack layout or scheduling, even if they are semantically unused.
Makefile migration from asm to src should be done by replacing the specific object path, not by adding the C object while leaving the assembly object active.
naming_conventions: Known public or SDK-like names use canonical capitalization, e.g. HSD_PadRumbleOn, HSD_PadLibData, PADStatus, PADControlMotor.
Unknown functions retain address-based names such as func_803780DC, func_80378128, func_803786F0, and func_80378828.
Unknown data labels retain address-based names such as lbl_804C22E0 and lbl_80406DE0.
Temporary or inferred structs use placeholder names such as Struct804C22E0 and retain unk-prefixed fields for unidentified members.
Struct typedefs follow HSD-style names where a domain meaning is known or partially known, e.g. HSD_PadRumbleListData and HSD_PadData.
Helper functions apparently corresponding to inlined code paths are suffixed with '_inline', e.g. func_80378330_inline, func_80378430_inline, and func_803786F0_inline.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.o with src/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.o in TEXT_O_FILES to make the decompiled C object participate in the main build.
Added rumble.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, indicating this object still required epilogue handling in that build path.
Used offset-annotated struct layouts to match memory accesses precisely.
Used pointer-to-pointer linked-list traversal to reproduce compact list unlink/insert code.
Used BOOL locals holding OSDisableInterrupts return values to preserve interrupt restore behavior and likely register lifetimes.
Used dummy stack arrays in functions with controller loops to influence stack layout.
Left some typed pointers as u16* with comments noting intended HSD_Rumble* typing, suggesting exact codegen was favored over ideal type clarity.
Represented the rumble bytecode decoder with bit extraction from the high bits of the current list word: switch ((*(u8 *)a->listp >> 5) & 7).
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Because there is no review discussion, no specific reviewer requests or rationale can be attributed.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80378828 to HSD_PadRumbleInit or move rumble structs into shared headers.
Search for later cleanup of Struct804C22E0, lbl_804C22E0, and lbl_80406DE0 names.
Check whether HSD_PadRumbleListData and PadLibData were later centralized with controller or padlib definitions.
Look for later PRs that remove dummy[8] stack-padding locals from rumble.c after compiler or matching improvements.
Search for documentation or naming of the rumble command opcodes handled in func_80378560.
